label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about what can be concluded from Isabelle Dinoire's remarks. First, I need to look at her actual quotes in the text. The text says that after seeing her face in the mirror for the first time, she was scared but thought it was "marvelous" and "couldn't believe it". She also said it was already beautiful, even though she expected it to be blue and swollen. Then later, less than a year after the operation, she says "I have been saved" and that people told her it's wonderful, calling it a miracle. Looking at the choices: A) She was too shocked to see herself in the mirror. But in the text, she was scared, but she did look and was positive. So maybe not A. B) She was satisfied with the result of the operation. Her comments about it being marvelous, beautiful, and calling it a miracle suggest she's satisfied. The doctors also mentioned she's living a normal life again. So B seems likely. C) She thought her damaged face couldn't be repaired. The text says doctors offered little hope, but she didn't comment on that. She was actually surprised it was repaired. So C is probably not correct. D) She looked more beautiful than before.
